excerpt: A decline in the general level of prices for goods and services in an economy.

title: Delayed Proof of Work (dPoW)
excerpt: Delayed Proof of Work (dPoW) is a second-layer consensus security mechanism, designed to protect blockchains from 51% attacks threatening the integrity of the network.

title: Delegated Proof-of-Stake (dPOS)
excerpt: An alternative to the Proof-of-Stake and Proof-of-Work consensus algorithms.

title: Delisting
excerpt: The process of removing an asset/stock/cryptocurrency from a trading exchange is called delisting.

title: Demurrage
excerpt: Demurrage is a fee charged for using an asset beyond a certain time period.

title: Dencun Upgrade
excerpt: Dencun is essentially the fusion of two separate upgrades - 'Deneb' and ‘Cancun’ together targeting improvements across Ethereum's consensus and execution layers.

title: Denial-of-Service (DoS) Attack
excerpt: A denial-of-service attack aims to temporarily make a computer or network service unavailable to its intended users.

title: DePIN
excerpt: Decentralized Physical Infrastructure Network, or DePIN is an innovative approach that combines the power of blockchain with real-world physical infrastructure to create decentralized, efficient, and community-driven networks.

title: Depth Chart
excerpt: A graph that plots the requests to buy (bids) and the requests to sell (asks) on a chart, based on limit orders. The chart shows the point at which the market is most likely to accept a transaction.

title: Derivative
excerpt: A financial instrument deriving its value from the value of an underlying asset.

title: Derivatives Market
excerpt: A public market for derivatives, instruments such as futures contracts or options, which are derived from other forms of cryptocurrency assets.

title: Desktop Wallet
excerpt: A desktop wallet is a type of software wallet that is usually non-custodial.

title: Deterministic Wallet
excerpt: A type of cryptocurrency wallet in which keys and addresses are created from a single seed.

title: Dex Aggregator
excerpt: DEX aggregators are a relatively new type of blockchain-based service that allow cryptocurrency traders to benefit from a large variety of financial tools in a single interface, often providing better liquidity and prices on different crypto pairs.

title: Dharma Protocol
excerpt: An open-source stack for building debt markets on Ethereum.

title: Diamond Hands
excerpt: Diamond Hands is a popular term on social media platforms. It refers to people who hold their coins even if their portfolio drops in value by more than 20%.

title: Difficulty
excerpt: A measure of how hard it is to validate a new block on a blockchain.

title: Digital
excerpt: Digital technologies are these electronic tools that have the ability to generate, store or even process data.

title: Digital Art
excerpt: Digital art is art and media that is made by using digital technology.

title: Digital Asset
excerpt: A digital asset refers to the digital representation of something of value.

title: Digital Asset Custodian
excerpt: A digital asset custodian is responsible to look after digital assets on behalf of an investor or client.

title: Digital Asset Ecosystem
excerpt: Digital asset ecosystem is a term that defines everything involved in the crypto space. From NFTs to futures, this terms sums up all the facilities offered and elements associated with the crypto universe.

title: Digital Barter Economy
excerpt: Eliminating the inherent weaknesses of the traditional barter economy, a digital barter economy makes it easier to trade both physical and virtual items anywhere in the world.

title: Digital Commodity
excerpt: A commodity that exists digitally, as opposed to in “meatspace.”

title: Digital Currency
excerpt: A currency that exists only in digital form, as opposed to traditional physical currencies.

title: Digital Dollar
excerpt: The term "digital dollar" refers to a possible digital currency issued by the US central bank (CBDC).

title: Digital Identity
excerpt: Information used by a person or entity to identify themselves to a computer or network.

title: Digital Signature
excerpt: A method for proving the authenticity of a digital communication.

title: Digital Signature Algorithm (DSA)
excerpt: Digital Signature Algorithm (DSA) is a signature algorithm, not an encryption algorithm, and uses public-key cryptography to generate digital signatures.

title: Dildo
excerpt: No, not that. The red or green “candles,” or vertical lines, on graphs showing cryptocurrency market data.

title: Dip
excerpt: A dip is when markets experience a short or protracted downturn.

title: Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG)
excerpt: A way of structuring data, often used for data modelling, and increasingly as a consensus tool in cryptocurrencies.

title: Discord
excerpt: Discord is a web-based communication tool or application primarily built to enable communication between gamers.

title: Distributed Consensus
excerpt: Collective agreement reached among nodes in a network.

title: Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) Attack
excerpt: An attempt by a bad actor to disrupt the operation of an application, server or network by flooding it with traffic.

title: Distributed Ledger
excerpt: Distributed ledgers are ledgers in which data is stored across a network of decentralized nodes. A distributed ledger does not necessarily involve a cryptocurrency and may be permissioned and private.

title: Distributed Ledger Technology (DLT)
excerpt: A database that is shared by multiple participants, in multiple places. The basis for blockchains.

title: Distributed Network
excerpt: A network in which the data and applications are dependent on multiple sources, as opposed to one location.

title: Distributed Validator Technology (DVT)
excerpt: DVT operates as a security system for staking validators. It spreads key management and signs tasks across multiple parties.

title: Distribution Phase
excerpt: The distribution phase is the opposite of the accumulation phase. In this phase, the market moves sideways and is range-bound after experiencing an extended uptrend.

title: Diversification
excerpt: Diversification is a risk-management strategy that mixes a wide variety of investments within a portfolio.

title: Diversified Proof of Stake
excerpt: Diversified Proof of Stake is a variation of the popular PoS consensus mechanism that allows multiple assets to be staked on a single blockchain.

title: Documentation
excerpt: Documentation is a part of token economies that stores all the details of an asset on the blockchain.

title: Dolphin
excerpt: Someone with a moderate holding of cryptocurrency.

title: Dominance
excerpt: A measure of Bitcoin's value in the context of the larger cryptocurrency market.

title: Dorian Nakamoto
excerpt: Dorian Nakamoto is a Japanese-American physicist who some believe to be Satoshi Nakamoto.

title: DotSama
excerpt: DotSama is a new piece of crypto slang, used to describe the Kusama and Polkadot ecosystems in just one word.

title: Double Spend Attack
excerpt: A double-spend attack is a practice in the world of digital currencies where a user gains the ability to spend the same cryptocurrency more than once.

title: Double Spending
excerpt: The potential for a digital currency to be spent twice.

title: dPoSec (Distributed Proof of Security)
excerpt: dPoSec (Distributed Proof of Security) is a consensus mechanism designed to ensure that the blockchain network continues to operate even if a third of the nodes are compromised. It addresses the key challenges faced by the existing distributed network of nodes and validators.

title: Drawdown
excerpt: The maximum reduction in value from the peak value for an investment or fund that has occurred over a period of time.

title: DRC-20
excerpt: DRC-20 is a token standard on the Dogecoin network that allows developers and users to create fungible assets within Dogecoin's ecosystem. It is similar to ERC-20 on Ethereum.

title: Drivechain
excerpt: Drivechain is a Bitcoin improvement proposal that aims to scale Bitcoin and add new features using sidechains.

title: Dual Governance
excerpt: Dual governance refers to a two-pronged system of decision-making in a decentralized autonomous organization (DAO), where two distinct parties are involved in running the organization rather than one larger body.

title: Dual-Token Economy/Model (Two-Token Economy)
excerpt: In the world of blockchain, a dual-token economy or model means a project with two tokens, one of which is used for utility inside the network and the other one as security to raise funds for the crypto project.

title: Dump
excerpt: A sudden sell-off of digital assets.

title: Dumping
excerpt: A collective market sell-off that occurs when large quantities of a particular cryptocurrency are sold in a short period of time.

title: Dust Transactions
excerpt: Miniscule amounts of Bitcoin in a wallet — with a value that would be outweighed by the cost of a transaction fee.

title: Dusting Attack
excerpt: An attack that aims to uncover the identity of a wallet’s owner, information that can subsequently be used in phishing scams.

title: DYCO (Dynamic Coin Offering)
excerpt: DYCO (dynamic coin offering) is a new crowdfunding model developed by DAO Maker that employs utility tokens that are backed by USD.

title: DYOR
excerpt: The acronym of Do Your Own Research — encouraging investors to complete due diligence into a project before investing.

title: E-Signature
excerpt: An electronic signature, or e-signature, is any electronic mark (sign, sound, symbol, etc.) used in place of a physical signature in signing a document or contract.

title: Economic Utility
excerpt: Economic utility is a term in economics that refers to the total satisfaction that a person can derive from consuming a good or service.

title: Edge Nodes
excerpt: In computer science, an edge node is a computer that serves as an end-user gateway to form a connection with other nodes.

title: Effective Proof-of-Stake
excerpt: Effective Proof-of-Stake is Harmony’s version of the Proof-of-Stake consensus mechanism that aims for both security and decentralization.

title: EIP-1559
excerpt: EIP-1559 is an upgrade to the Ethereum network that simplified the fee market mechanism.

title: Electrum Wallet
excerpt: A Bitcoin wallet for Windows, macOS and Linux with a simple interface.

title: ELI5
excerpt: Short for “explain like I’m five” — a plea for simplicity when crypto concepts are being explained.

title: Elliott Waves
excerpt: The Elliott Wave Theory is an essential tool for many stock and crypto market traders.

title: EMA (Exponential Moving Average)
excerpt: Exponential moving average (EMA) is a technical indicator that highlights the recent price changes and data points of an asset/stock/cryptocurrency while keeping the older chart observations intact.

title: Email Spoofing
excerpt: Email spoofing is a technique that is used in order to trick users into thinking that a message actually came from a different person.

title: Emission
excerpt: The speed at which new coins are produced and released.

title: Encryption
excerpt: Encryption is a method through which information can be made into code.

title: Enterprise Blockchain
excerpt: Enterprise blockchain is the use of distributed ledger technology for non-speculative business purposes. Tailored for the needs of enterprises, these chains may be private or public.

title: Enterprise Ethereum Alliance (EEA)
excerpt: A group of organizations and companies working together to further develop the Ethereum network.

title: Epoch
excerpt: One entire run of the training dataset through the algorithm is referred to as an epoch in machine learning.

title: Equity
excerpt: Equity is the funds that would be returned to a company's shareholders if all of the company's assets were dissolved and all debts were paid off in the event of liquidation.

title: Erasure Coding
excerpt: Erasure coding is a method of storing data at multiple locations after doing its segmentation, expansion, and encoding with redundant information.

title: ERC 7512
excerpt: ERC-7512 aims to standardize how audit reports are represented directly on Ethereum's blockchain.

title: ERC-1155
excerpt: ERC-1155 digital token standard was created by Enjin and offers more security in comparison to older token standards. It can be used to create both fungible and non-fungible assets on the Ethereum network.

title: ERC-20
excerpt: Tokens designed and used solely on the Ethereum platform.

title: ERC-223
excerpt: ERC-223 is an Ethereum token standard that is powered by smart contracts that enable users to securely transfer tokens to a digital wallet.

title: ERC-721
excerpt: A token standard for non-fungible Ethereum tokens.

title: ERC-777
excerpt: ERC-777 is a tradable token standard spun out from ERC-20 to enable a new way to engage with a token contract while staying backward compatible.

title: ERC-827
excerpt: ERC-827 is an ETH token standard that addresses the existing limitations of ERC 20 when it comes to the implementation of calls in transfers and approvals in particular.

title: ERC-884
excerpt: ERC-884 facilitates the creation of tradable ERC-20 tokens, each of which symbolizes a numberless share issued by a Delaware corporation.

title: ERC-948
excerpt: ERC-948 is a new Ethereum token protocol that is designed to connect subscription businesses with customers and allows for subscription-based transactions.

title: Escrow
excerpt: A financial instrument where assets or cash are held by a third party while a buyer and a seller complete a deal.

title: Esports
excerpt: Electronic sports, commonly known as e-sports, is a term used for digital gaming competition, in which players battle against each other in an individual or team-based format often in a competition or event that offers huge monetary rewards to the winners. Some e-sports games are also available in a single-player mode.

title: ETH/BTC
excerpt: ETH/BTC is a popular cryptocurrency trading pair that denominates the price of Ethereum in Bitcoin.

title: Ethash
excerpt: Ethash is the algorithm utilized for the proof of work mining Ethereum and ETH-based cryptocurrencies.

title: Ether
excerpt: The form of payment used in the operation of the distribution application platform, Ethereum.

title: Ethereum Difficulty
excerpt: Ethereum difficulty plays a crucial role in maintaining the stability and security of the network, especially in the context of Ethereum's transition to a Proof-of-Stake (PoS) consensus mechanism.

title: Ethereum ETF
excerpt: An Ethereum Exchange Traded Fund (ETF) is an investment tool that allows investors to get exposed to Ethereum's price movements through traditional stock exchanges.

title: Ethereum Improvement Proposal (EIP)
excerpt: Ethereum Improvement Proposals (EIPs) describe standards for the Ethereum platform, including core protocol specifications, client APIs, and contract standards.

title: Ethereum Request For Comment (ERC)
excerpt: Ethereum Request for Comment (ERC) is the protocol to introduce new improvements to the network by developers.

title: Ethereum Transaction
excerpt: Ethereum transaction are cryptographically signed instructions to initiate a transaction to update the state of the Ethereum network.

title: Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM)
excerpt: A Turing-complete virtual machine that enables execution of code exactly as intended; it is the runtime environment for every smart contract. Every Ethereum node runs on the EVM to maintain consensus across the blockchain.

title: Event Triggers
excerpt: When a transaction is mined, smart contracts can emit events and write logs to the blockchain which the frontend can then process.

title: Exchange
excerpt: Businesses that allow customers to trade cryptocurrencies for fiat money or other cryptocurrencies.

title: Exchange Traded Fund (ETF)
excerpt: A security that tracks a basket of assets such as stocks, bonds, and cryptocurrencies but can be traded like a single stock.

title: Exit Scam
excerpt: An exit scam is a trick where projects disappear (or shut down) after accumulating investors’ money. In other industries, this event occurs when a business stops shipping orders even if it is receiving payment for the new ones.

title: Fair AI
excerpt: Fair AI is a new approach to artificial intelligence (AI) that has an emphasis on decentralization and equitability through rewarding individuals for their data and compute contributions.

title: Fakeout
excerpt: A fakeout is a sudden and temporary move in a market that tricks traders into thinking that a trend is emerging when, in fact, it is not.

title: Falling Knife
excerpt: A falling knife refers to the price dive of an asset and denotes a downward momentum of the financial market.

title: Falling Wedge
excerpt: Falling wedges, also known as descending wedges, have a distinct downward slope and a bullish bias in comparison to symmetrical triangles, which have no discernible slope and no bias.

title: Fan Token
excerpt: A fan token is a cryptocurrency issued by a specific sports team and allows its holders to participate in the governing activities and attain exclusive rewards & discounts.

title: FATF Travel Rule
excerpt: The FATF Travel Rule requires virtual asset service providers to regulate information sharing for certain large transactions.

title: Faucet
excerpt: A cryptocurrency reward system usually on a website or app, that rewards users for completing certain tasks.

title: Fee Tiers
excerpt: Fee tiers refer to the fee structure that determines the amount charged when investors deposit or withdraw money and execute trades on a crypto exchange.

title: Fiat
excerpt: Fiat currency is “legal tender” backed by a central government, such as the Federal Reserve, with its own banking system, such as fractional reserve banking. It can take the form of physical cash, or it can be represented electronically, such as with bank credit.

title: Fiat On-Ramp
excerpt: A fiat-on ramp is a way to get cryptocurrency from fiat, or regular money.

title: Fiat-Pegged Cryptocurrency
excerpt: A coin, token or asset issued on a blockchain that is linked to a government or bank-issued currency.

title: Fibonacci Retracement Level
excerpt: The Fibonacci retracement method uses a set of key numbers called Fibonacci ratios to identify the support and resistance levels of an asset/stock/cryptocurrency.

title: Field Programmable Gate Array
excerpt: Field Programmable Gate Array is an integrated circuit that allows customers or designers to reconfigure as per requirement after the manufacturing process.

title: Financial Action Task Force (FATF)
excerpt: The FATF is a global organization that sets global standards to combat money laundering and terrorist financing (AML/CFT).

title: Financial Crime Enforcement Network (FinCEN)
excerpt: The Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) is a federal regulatory bureau of the United States Treasury.

title: Financial Transactions and Reports Analysis Centre (FINTRAC)
excerpt: The Financial Transactions and Reports Analysis Centre (FINTRAC) of Canada is the nation’s financial intelligence agency.

title: First In, First Out
excerpt: First in, First Out (FIFO) is an inventory method used to specify your cost-basis when calculating your taxes.

title: First-Mover Advantage (FMA)
excerpt: The first-mover advantage refers to the launch of an innovative product or service which provides a head-start to a company by creating brand loyalty and penetrating markets before their future competitors.

title: Fish
excerpt: Someone who has a small crypto investment.

title: Flash Crash
excerpt: A flash crash is a market condition where an asset’s price falls very rapidly within a very brief time interval.

title: Flash Loan
excerpt: A flash loan is a transaction in which a specific quantity of liquidity is borrowed and repaid in the same transaction or block.

title: Flash Loan Attack
excerpt: Flash loan attacks are when malicious actors exploit a smart contract.

title: Flash Loans
excerpt: Flash loans are a type of uncollateralized lending used in decentralized finance (DeFi).

title: Flashbots
excerpt: Flashbots was launched as an independent research and development organization with an aim to lessen the adverse effects of the Maximal Extractable Value (MEV) extraction.

title: Flatcoin
excerpt: Flatcoins are cryptocurrencies whose value is pegged to the cost of living, rather than fiat or commodity.

title: Flippening
excerpt: A hypothetical scenario where Ethereum's market cap overtakes Bitcoin's.

title: Flipping
excerpt: An investment strategy where you buy something with the goal of reselling for a profit later, usually in a short period of time.

title: FOMO
excerpt: An acronym that stands for "Fear of Missing Out."

title: Fork (Blockchain)
excerpt: Forks, or chain splits, create an alternate version of the blockchain, leaving two blockchains to run simultaneously.

title: Fork (Software)
excerpt: When an entirely new program has been developed from source code, taken from an open source software.

title: Fork Choice Rule
excerpt: The fork choice rule is a mechanism in Ethereum that allows nodes to agree on a canonical chain when the network splits into competing forks.

title: Fractional Stablecoins
excerpt: A fractional stablecoin is one that is backed in two ways: collaterally-backed and algorithmically modified.

title: Fraud Proof
excerpt: A fraud proof is a technological method that functions as a bond in a decentralized environment that uses Optimistic Rollups (ORs), which are sidechains that aim to reduce the costs and latency that dApps might encounter on a blockchain platform.

title: Front Running
excerpt: Front running is when you place a transaction in a queue when you have knowledge of a future transaction.

title: FUD [2021]
excerpt: An acronym that stands for “Fear, Uncertainty and Doubt.” It is a strategy to influence perception of certain cryptocurrencies or the cryptocurrency market in general by spreading negative, misleading or false information. *see FUDster.

title: FUDster
excerpt: Someone that is spreading FUD.

title: Full Node
excerpt: Nodes that download a blockchain’s entire history in order to observe and enforce its rules.

title: Fully Diluted Value (FDV)
excerpt: FDV is the total worth or market cap of a cryptocurrency if the entire supply of tokens were in circulation.

title: Fully Homomorphic Encryption
excerpt: Fully homomorphic encryption is a type of scheme where one can perform arbitrary computations on encrypted data and generate the same results as when performing those computations on the plaintext.

title: Fundamental Analysis (FA)
excerpt: Fundamental analysis is a method of evaluating the underlying economic and financial factors that affect the value of an asset.

title: Funding Payments
excerpt: Funding payments are periodic payments between traders. These are designed to reduce the discrepancy between the perpetual market price and the spot market price.

title: Fungible
excerpt: In cryptocurrency, fungibility is when a coin or token can be replaced by any other identical coin or token.

title: Fusion Rollups
excerpt: Fusion rollups are a blockchain scalability solution that combines the best of other L2 approaches such as Appchains and Shared Rollups.

title: Futo
excerpt: Futo is an organization that develops and invests in decentralized technologies and companies.

title: Futures
excerpt: A futures contract is a standardized legal agreement to buy or sell a particular commodity or asset at a predetermined price at a specified time in the future.

title: Gains
excerpt: Gains refer to an increase in value or profit.

title: Game Channels
excerpt: Game channels are the newest technological advancement in the world of blockchain gaming, as they enable fast gameplay by removing the wait time for block confirmations. With game channels, games and dApps can run off-chain securely, individually, and in near real-time.

title: Game Theory
excerpt: Game theory is a way of creating a simplified interactive environment (a ‘game’) that allows researchers to model how people and entities will respond to certain actions.

title: GameFi
excerpt: GameFi, better known as play-to-earn (P2E) games, is a rather new term in the field of both gaming and cryptocurrency industries. It references games that are designed with economic and financial aspects of blockchain and cryptocurrencies, enabling players to exert full control over their in-game assets to generate revenue.

title: Gas
excerpt: A term used on the Ethereum platform that refers to a unit of measuring the computational effort of conducting transactions or smart contracts, or launch DApps in the Ethereum network. It is the “fuel” of the Ethereum network. *see Gas Limit and Gas Price.

title: Gas Limit
excerpt: A term used on the Ethereum platform that refers to the maximum amount of gas the user is willing to spend on a transaction.

title: Gas Price
excerpt: A term used on the Ethereum platform that refers to the price you are willing to pay for a transaction.

title: Gas Station Networks (GSN)
excerpt: Gas Station Networks (GSN) allows you to build decentralized applications (dApps) which provide payment for transactions, so users do not need to hold Ether or ETH to pay for gas, easing their onboarding process and improving user acquisition and experience.

title: Gavin Wood
excerpt: Gavin Wood is the co-founder of Parity Technologies, and one of the founders of Ethereum.

title: Gems
excerpt: Gem is a term for relatively unknown low-cap coins that have immense potential or are grossly undervalued.

title: Genesis Block
excerpt: The first block of data that is processed and validated to form a new blockchain, often referred to as block 0 or block 1.

title: Geotagged NFT
excerpt: Geotagged non-fungible tokens (NFT) feature 3D versions of the street art alongside the corresponding geo-location. They allows art aficionados to own both the virtual and physical artwork without the need to remove the actual infrastructure it was originally painted on.

title: Geth
excerpt: Geth, short for Go Ethereum, is a command-line interface that allows developers to run full Ethereum nodes, mine the cryptocurrency and execute smart contracts.

title: GitHub
excerpt: GitHub is one of the most popular code hosting platforms, allowing developers to collaborate on various projects.

title: Goguen Phase
excerpt: The Goguen phase of Cardano allows the development of smart contracts and DApps.

title: Gold-Backed Cryptocurrency
excerpt: A coin or token issued that represents a value of gold; for example, one physical gram of gold equals one coin.

title: Golden Cross
excerpt: A golden cross is a bullish technical trading indicator that emerges when the 50-day moving average crosses the 200-day moving average, signaling an imminent price rise of the asset/stock/cryptocurrency.

title: Google Authenticator
excerpt: Google Authenticator is a software-based verification system that generates unique one-time codes that are time-based on your mobile phone.

title: Governance
excerpt: In the world of cryptocurrencies, governance is defined as the people or organizations that have decision-making powers regarding the project.

title: Governance Token
excerpt: A governance token is a token that can be used to vote on decisions that influence an ecosystem.

title: GPG Encryption
excerpt: GNU Privacy Guard (GPG) is a popular OpenPGP (Pretty Good Privacy) implementation. It is an open-source alternative to PGP that encrypts and signs communications and data securely.

title: Graphical Processing Unit (GPU)
excerpt: More commonly known as a graphics card, it is a computer chip that creates 3D images on computers, but has turned out to be efficient for mining cryptocurrencies.

title: Gray Swan Event
excerpt: Gray swan is a significant event whose occurrence could be predicted, but the likelihood is low.

title: Greater Fool Theory
excerpt: The greater fool theory was first discussed by professor Burton Malkiel. It suggests that there is always a “greater fool” that you can sell an overvalued asset to.

title: Green Candle
excerpt: A green candle is an indication of the price closing higher than the opening price. The green candle is an indication that the overall sentiment of the market at the time of trading was bullish, or positive. A wide body with a small tail on top indicates a strong bullish movement in the market.

title: Group Mining
excerpt: As opposed to solo mining, group mining is when multiple people mine together.

title: Gwei
excerpt: The denomination used in defining the cost of gas in transactions involving Ether.

title: Hacking
excerpt: Hacking is the process of using a computer to manipulate another computer or computer system in an unauthorized fashion.

title: Hal Finney
excerpt: Hal Finney was a cryptographer and programmer who pioneered Bitcoin’s development and worked with Satoshi Nakamoto.

title: Hard Cap
excerpt: A hard cap is the absolute maximum supply of a digital asset.

title: Hard Fork (Blockchain)
excerpt: A type of protocol change that validates all previously invalid transactions, and invalidates all previously valid transactions.

title: Hard Fork Combinator
excerpt: A hard fork combinator (first designed by IOHK) is a tool to combine protocols specifically on the Cardano blockchain after a hard fork has occurred.

title: Hard Peg
excerpt: A hard peg is an exchange rate policy, where a currency is set at a fixed rate against another currency.

title: Hardware Security Module
excerpt: A hardware security module is a type of computing device that secures digital keys and encrypts data.

title: Hardware Wallet
excerpt: A hardware wallet is a wallet for cryptocurrencies that usually resemble a USB stick.

title: Hash
excerpt: A hash is the output result of a hashing algorithm, which creates a unique, fixed-length string to encrypt and secure a certain selection of arbitrary data.

title: Hash Function
excerpt: Any function used to map data of arbitrary size to data of a fixed size. *see Cryptographic Hash Function.

title: Hash Power / Hash Rate
excerpt: A unit of measurement for the amount of computing power being consumed by the network to continuously operate.

title: Hashed Timelock Contract (HTLC)
excerpt: A hashed timelock contract (HTLC) is an agreement between two parties that requires no trust between two users by offering special features to reduce risk.

title: Hashgraph Consensus Mechanism
excerpt: The hashgraph consensus is an advanced and up-to-date version of the technology that enables consensus mechanisms.

title: Haskell Programming Language
excerpt: The Haskell programming language is a standardized, general-purpose, statically-typed, purely functional programming language that came into existence in 1990.

title: Hedge Contract
excerpt: A hedge contract is a form of insurance that investors use to hedge against the risk of financial loss. Typically, a hedge is designed to protect against price fluctuations in the market.

title: Hedge Fund
excerpt: A hedge fund is pooled investment fund that employs a number of investment strategies in a variety of liquid asset classes.

title: Hidden Cap
excerpt: Hidden cap is an unknown limit to the amount of money a team elects to receive from investors in its initial coin offering (ICO). The purpose of a hidden cap is to even the playing field by letting smaller investors put in money, without the large investors forming an accurate understanding of the total cap and adjusting their investment as a result.

title: Hierarchical Deterministic Wallet (HD Wallet)
excerpt: A wallet that uses Hierarchical Deterministic (HD) protocol to support the generation of crypto-wallets from a single master seed using 12 mnemonic phrases. *see Deterministic Wallet.

title: Higher High
excerpt: A higher high is when the price of a cryptocurrency closes higher than the previous day, which itself closed at a high.

title: Higher Low
excerpt: A higher low is when the price of a cryptocurrency closes at a level that is higher than the close of the previous day.

title: HODL
excerpt: A type of passive investment strategy where you hold an investment for a long period of time, regardless of any changes in the price or markets. The term first became famous due to a typo made in a Bitcoin forum, and the term is now commonly expanded to stand for “Hold On for Dear Life.”

title: Honeyminer
excerpt: Honeyminer is a cryptocurrency mining app available for download on multiple devices.

title: Honeypot
excerpt: A honeypot is a scam used in the crypto industry to trap victims and steal their assets or sensitive information.

title: Hostage Byte Attack
excerpt: A Hostage Byte Attack is a distributed denial of service (DDoS) attack against a user that stored its data on a malicious storage node and is asked to pay ransom to retrieve its data.

title: Hosted Wallet
excerpt: A wallet managed by a third-party service.

title: Hot Storage
excerpt: The online storage of private keys allowing for quicker access to cryptocurrencies. *see Cold Storage.

title: Hot Wallet
excerpt: A cryptocurrency wallet that is connected to the internet for hot storage of cryptoassets.

title: Howey Test
excerpt: A test used to determine whether or not an asset is a security.

title: Human Keys
excerpt: Human Keys are cryptographic keys derived from what you are, what you know, or what you have. They are used to secure digital assets, protect privacy, and access the decentralized web.

title: Human-Readable Names
excerpt: In computer programming, human-readable refers to making information able to be read naturally by humans and stands in opposition to machine-readable format - i.e binary.

title: Huobi BTC (HBTC)
excerpt: Launched by Huobi, Huobi BTC or HBTC is a standard ERC-20 token pegged to BTC on a 1:1 ratio.

title: Hybrid PoW/PoS
excerpt: A hybrid PoW/PoS allows for both proof-of-stake and proof-of-work as consensus distribution algorithms on the network. This approach aims to bring together the security of PoW consensus and the governance and energy efficiency of PoS.

title: Hydra (Cardano)
excerpt: Hydra is a layer-two scaling solution for the Cardano blockchain that aims to increase the transaction processing capacity of the network by allowing multiple heads or channels.

title: Hyperinflation
excerpt: Hyperinflation is defined as unrestricted growth in prices for goods and services in an economy. It happens when resources become limited, such as gas or food, and prices rise as demand outstrips supply.

title: Hyperledger (Hyperledger Foundation)
excerpt: Hyperledger is an umbrella project of open source blockchains and blockchain-related tools started by the Linux Foundation in 2015 to support the collaborative development of blockchain-based distributed ledgers.

title: Immutable
excerpt: A property that defines the inability to be changed, especially over time.

title: Impermanent Loss
excerpt: Impermanent loss is when a liquidity provider has a temporary loss of funds because of volatility in a trading pair.

title: In-the-Money / Out-of-the-Money
excerpt: In-the-money and out-of-the-money are options trading mechanisms that allow investors to benefit from additional tools to work with the market.

title: Infinite Approval
excerpt: Pre-approving smart contracts to enable the platform to spend any amount of your coins.

title: Infinite Mint Attack
excerpt: An infinite mint attack occurs when an unwanted entity or hacker mints an absurd ("infinite") amount of tokens within a protocol.

title: Inflation
excerpt: A general increase in prices and fall in the purchasing value of money.

title: Initial Bounty Offering (IBO)
excerpt: A novel way of launching a project that focuses on people contributing skills to a platform rather than money.

title: Initial Coin Offering (ICO)
excerpt: Short for Initial Coin Offering, an ICO is a type of crowdfunding, or crowdsale, using cryptocurrencies as a means of raising capital for early-stage companies.

title: Initial Dex Offering (IDO)
excerpt: An initial dex offering (IDX) is an alternative to an initial coin offering (ICO).

title: Initial Exchange Offering
excerpt: A type of crowdfunding where crypto start-ups generate capital by listing through an exchange.

title: Initial Farm Offering (IFO)
excerpt: Initial Farm Offering (IFO) helps DeFi projects raise capital through the farming feature offered by decentralized exchanges.

title: Initial Game Offering (IGO)
excerpt: Initial game offerings (IGOs) provide individuals with an opportunity to invest in gaming projects at an early stage that have the potential to offer huge returns after their launch.

title: Initial NFT Offering (INO)
excerpt: A crypto crowdfunding solution where projects can raise funds by listing a set of NFTs via a launchpad.

title: Initial Public Offering (IPO)
excerpt: An initial public offering (IPO) is the process of a company offering shares for purchase on the stock market for the first time.

title: Initial Stake Pool Offering (ISPO)
excerpt: The Initial Stake Pool Offering (ISPO) is a comparatively new crypto fundraising method exclusive to the Cardano ecosystem. Unlike existing fundraising models, ISPOs are more inclusive, decentralized, equitable, and secure.

title: Initial Token Offering (ITO)
excerpt: ITOs are similar to initial coin offerings — but have more of a focus on offering tokens with intrinsic utility in the form of software or usage in an ecosystem.

title: Input-Output Hong Kong (IOHK)
excerpt: Now Input-Output Global, Input-Output Hong Kong (IOHK) was launched in 2015 by Charles Hoskinson to provide a blockchain infrastructure to research & engineering companies.

title: Insider Trading
excerpt: Insider trading happens when someone purchases or sells stocks while possessing private, material information about that stock.

title: Instamine
excerpt: When a large portion of a coin’s total supply is distributed to investors shortly after launch.

title: Instant Settlement Network Layer
excerpt: Instant settlement network allows participants to exchange digital assets in real-time from anywhere in the world.

title: Institutional Investor
excerpt: An Institutional Investor is an organization or a legal entity that trades in the market on behalf of its clients that may be retail investors.

title: Insurance Fund
excerpt: An exchange insurance fund is used to cover any unexpected losses from leveraged trading. This fund is used to prevent traders from bankruptcy in the event of liquidations.

title: Integrated Application
excerpt: Integrated Applications combine the advantages of appchains (customizability, performance, interoperability) and smart contracts (composability, standardization, minimal overhead) to enable developers to build better applications that can onboard users from anywhere.

title: Integrated Development Environment (IDE)
excerpt: An integrated development environment (IDE) is a type of software that helps you develop apps by merging many development tools into a single graphical user interface (GUI).

title: Intellectual Property (IP)
excerpt: Intellectual property (IP) is a type of property that can be legally protected from being copied or sold – it includes intangible creations that result from human thinking such as a book, song, design, business method, or software.

title: Inter-Blockchain Communication (IBC)
excerpt: Inter-Blockchain Communication (IBC) is a communication protocol that allows different blockchains to relay messages to each other.

title: Intercontinental Exchange (ICE)
excerpt: The Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) is an American company founded in 2000 to purchase and operate global exchanges and clearing houses.

title: Interest Rates
excerpt: A time-dependent charge or return made in proportion to the amount of money deposited, borrowed or lent.

title: Intermediary/Middleman
excerpt: A person or entity that acts as the go-between different parties to bring about agreements or carry out directives.

title: Internal Transaction
excerpt: An internal transaction, also known as a "message," is a byproduct of an EOA interaction with a contract address that results in Ether being transferred.

title: Internet Layer
excerpt: Internet layer is the third layer in the TCP/IP model and is responsible for the transportation of network packets.

title: Internet Memes
excerpt: A meme is an image, a video, or a piece of text that is copied and spread rapidly by internet users. They are typically humorous but can also be critical as well.

title: Internet of Things
excerpt: Internet of Things (IoT) is a global interconnected network of devices, sensors and software that can collect and exchange data with each other in real-time over the Internet.

title: Internet Service Provider (ISP)
excerpt: Internet Service Providers (ISPs) are commercial entities that provide end-users with access to the internet.

title: Interoperability
excerpt: Blockchain interoperability, or cross-chain interoperability, is the ability to see and share information across multiple blockchains.

title: InterPlanetary File System (IPFS)
excerpt: The InterPlatery File System is a peer-to-peer, distributed system for storing and accessing files, as well as websites and applications, which relies on content addressing rather than location.

title: Intrinsic Value
excerpt: The intrinsic value of an asset depicts the asset’s actual worth based on a complex financial calculation rather than its current price.

title: Invest
excerpt: Investing is when you put money in a financial scheme with the intent of making a gain.

title: Investment Vehicles (Crypto-tied)
excerpt: Investment vehicles are the assets (classes) in which investors put their money with hopes to increase the value of their portfolio in the future.

title: IOU
excerpt: An IOU, acronym for “I owe you,” is a document stating that one party owes a debt to another party.

title: IP Address
excerpt: IP addresses are unique numeric addresses assigned to devices connected to the internet or a local network.

title: Isolated Margin
excerpt: This mode is useful for traders who want to take speculative positions, and where there is a probable chance of their speculation turning out to be incorrect. In such a situation, the user will be better protected as compared to cross-margin mode because only their isolated margin balance will face liquidation, instead of their entire margin balance. The downside of using isolated margin is that your exposure will be limited to one position in a particular market.

title: Jager
excerpt: The smallest denomination of Binance Coin (BNB) is called Jager.

title: Java
excerpt: Java is a general-purpose, class-based as well as object-oriented programming language.

title: JavaScript
excerpt: JavaScript is a powerful, dynamic, lightweight, and advanced programming language. It is mostly used in web-based applications.

title: JOMO
excerpt: Joy of missing out (JOMO) is the opposite of having a fear of missing out (FOMO.)

title: Keylogger
excerpt: A keylogger or keystroke logging software is a spying tool often used by hackers to record keystrokes made by users.

title: Kimchi Premium
excerpt: Kimchi premium is a phenomenon occurring in South Korean crypto exchanges, making valuations appear higher than on other international exchanges.

title: Klinger Oscillator
excerpt: The Klinger volume oscillator is a volume-based technical indicator that compares volume to price to forecast price reversals in the financial markets.

title: Know Your Customer (KYC)
excerpt: Short for Know Your Customer, these are checks that crypto exchanges and trading platforms must complete to verify the identity of their customers.

title: Lachesis
excerpt: The consensus mechanism of Fantom blockchain.

title: Lambo
excerpt: Slang for the type of car that many crypto enthusiasts aspire to buy when their digital assets “moon” — or rise in value substantially.

title: Large Cap
excerpt: Well-established projects and organizations having a market capitalization of $10 billion or above are called large cap or big cap projects/organizations.

title: Laser Eyes
excerpt: Laser eyes is a viral Twitter meme that is used by Bitcoiners who attempt to push the price of BTC to its new all-time highs. It was originated with a hashtag, #LaserRayUntil100, back in February 2021.

title: Law of Accelerating Returns
excerpt: The Law of Accelerating Returns is a hypothesis by Ray Kurzweil based on the observations that technologies (or any evolutionary system) tend to progress in an exponential fashion.

title: Layer 0
excerpt: Layer 0 is a network framework running beneath the blockchain. It is made up of protocols, connections, hardware, miners, and everything else that forms the foundation of the blockchain ecosystem.

title: Layer 2
excerpt: Layer 2 is the name given to a scaling solution that enables high throughput of transactions whilst fully inheriting the security of the underlying blockchain that it is built on.

title: Layer-1 Blockchain
excerpt: A layer-1 blockchain is a set of solutions that improve the base protocol itself

title: Leased Proof of Stake (LPoS)
excerpt: Leased Proof of Stake (LPoS) is a consensus mechanism that allows cryptocurrency holders to lease their coins to nodes on a network.

title: Ledger
excerpt: A record of financial transactions that cannot be changed, only appended with new transactions.

title: Leverage
excerpt: Money that a trader borrows from a brokerage, enabling them to gain far greater exposure to a position than what their capital allows.

title: Leveraged Tokens
excerpt: In the world of cryptocurrencies, leveraged tokens give you a leveraged position in trading, meaning that your earnings and losses are multiplied.

title: libp2p
excerpt: Libp2p is an open network protocol for decentralized peer-to-peer networking.

title: Light Node
excerpt: Light nodes are typically downloaded wallets and are connected to full nodes to further validate the information that is stored on the blockchain.

title: Lightning Network
excerpt: A second-layer protocol that is designed to solve Bitcoin’s scalability problem by allowing transactions to be processed more quickly.

title: Limit Order
excerpt: A limit order is a type of order to purchase or sell a security at a specified price or a better one.

title: Limit Order/Limit Buy/Limit Sell
excerpt: Tools that enable traders to automatically buy or sell cryptocurrencies on a trading platform when a certain price target is reached.

title: LINK (Chainlink)
excerpt: LINK is an Ethereum-based token that is used to pay Chainlink node operators.

title: Liquid Market
excerpt: A liquid market features a large number of buyers and sellers. It is a platform where all the trades are executed with ease and at a low cost.

title: Liquid Proof of Stake (LPoS)
excerpt: Liquid proof of stake (LPoS) is an improvement over traditional proof of stake (PoS) that allows users to stake assets without fully locking them up.

title: Liquid Staking
excerpt: Liquid staking allows users to stake tokens and simultaneously use them in the DeFi ecosystem.

title: Liquid Staking (Fantom)
excerpt: A staking mechanism by the Fantom blockchain that allows you to earn yield by staking tokens for a longer duration.

title: Liquid Staking Derivatives
excerpt: Liquid Staking Derivatives (LSDs) are tokens that represent staked assets in a DeFi protocol.

title: Liquidation
excerpt: Liquidation refers to the conversion of an asset or cryptocurrency for fiat or its equivalents.

title: Liquidity
excerpt: Liquidity indicates how easy it is to convert a cryptocurrency into cash quickly — and whether this can be achieved without the asset’s value suffering.

title: Liquidity Bootstrapping Pool (LBP)
excerpt: A liquidity bootstrapping pool is essentially a contract that manages a core pool containing tokens to be used on an exchange.

title: Liquidity Hooks
excerpt: A Liquidity Hook is a highly composable plugin for liquidity, enabling protocols to seamlessly integrate with platforms for capital access and developing yield/trading strategies.

title: Liquidity Mining
excerpt: Liquidity mining is a mechanism or process in which participants supply cryptocurrencies into liquidity pools, and are rewarded with fees and tokens based on their share of the total pool liquidity.

title: Liquidity Pool
excerpt: Liquidity pools are crypto assets that are kept to facilitate the trading of trading pairs on decentralized exchanges.

title: Liquidity Provider
excerpt: Liquidity providers are decentralized exchange users who fund a liquidity pool with tokens they own.

title: Liquidity Provider Tokens (LP Tokens)
excerpt: Liquidity provider tokens or LP tokens are tokens issued to liquidity providers on a decentralized exchange (DEX) that run on an automated market maker (AMM) protocol.

title: Liveness
excerpt: A guarantee that a system will continue to provide data, and that no centralized authority can shut down its services.

title: LMD GHOST
excerpt: LMD GHOST (aka the GHOST Protocol) is a fork-choice rule that allows the nodes in a blockchain network to reach an agreement on the valid state of the ledger.

title: Loan-to-value (LTV)
excerpt: Used by lenders, loan-to-value (LTV) is the assessment ratio of risk involved while approving a loan. Simply, it is the ratio of the loan's value to the value of the collateral.

title: Location Swap
excerpt: Location swap allows the change of claim to the assets manifested in the form of a token with no effect on other attributes.

title: Long
excerpt: A situation where you buy a cryptocurrency with the expectation of selling it at a higher price for profit later.

title: Longing (Long Position)
excerpt: A long position (longing) refers to the situation where an investor buys a cryptocurrency or any other financial instrument to sell it later when the price goes high.

title: Lovelace
excerpt: The smallest denomination of ADA is called Lovelace.

title: Lower High
excerpt: A lower high is when the price of a cryptocurrency closes at a high but lower than the previous day.

title: Lower Low
excerpt: A lower low is when the price of a cryptocurrency closes lower than the previous day, which itself closed at a low.

title: Mainchain
excerpt: A mainchain is the base blockchain layer where all transactions are processed and finalized.

title: Mainnet
excerpt: An independent blockchain running its own network with its own technology and protocol.

title: Mainnet Swap
excerpt: Mainnet swap refers to the shift of a cryptocurrency project from one blockchain network to another (which in most cases is its own native blockchain network).

title: Maker Protocol (MakerDAO)
excerpt: The Maker Protocol allows users to leverage their assets as collateral that are approved by the Maker governance in order to get rewarded with DAI.

title: Malware
excerpt: Malware or malicious software refers to harmful programs utilized by bad actors to illegally access and/or compromise a computer, network or server.

title: Man-in-the-Middle Attack (MITM)
excerpt: a man-in-the-middle attack (MITM) attack is a general term for a cyberattack where a perpetrator positions himself in a conversation between two parties either to secretly eavesdrop.

title: Margin Call
excerpt: When an investor’s account value falls below the margin maintenance amount.

title: Margin Trading
excerpt: A practice where a trader uses borrowed funds from a broker to trade a cryptocurrency.

title: Market
excerpt: An area or arena, online or offline, in which commercial dealings are conducted.

title: Market Balances
excerpt: Market balances refer to the outstanding amount of tokens or coins after a trade has been made on a decentralized exchange (DEX).

title: Market Capitalization/Market Cap/MCAP
excerpt: Total capitalization of a cryptocurrency’s price. It is one of the ways to rank the relative size of a cryptocurrency. *see Circulating Supply.

title: Market Maker, Market Taker
excerpt: The maker places an order (to buy or sell at a quoted price), while a taker accepts that placed order (to execute the buy or sell at the quoted price)

title: Market Making as a Service (MMaaS)
excerpt: Market Making as a Service (MMaaS) is a technology service that enables token issuers to set their strategies in market making, allowing them to trade and manage their own liquidity.

title: Market Order/Market Buy/Market Sell
excerpt: A purchase or sale of a cryptocurrency on an exchange at the current best available price.

title: Market Signal
excerpt: Through signaling, market participants are essentially creating a volatile market which can help to point out the opportunities to the investors.

title: Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA)
excerpt: The Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation is a comprehensive regulatory framework instituted by the European Union (EU) to govern the issuance, trading, and provision of services related to crypto.

title: Marlowe
excerpt: Developed by Input Output Hong Kong (IOHK), Marlowe is an easy-to-use programming language for experts with no programming expertise or knowledge, enabling them to write smart contracts for financial products.

title: Masternodes
excerpt: Masternodes are a server maintained by its owner, somewhat like full nodes, but with additional functionalities such as anonymizing transactions, clearing transactions, and participating in governance and voting. It was initially popularized by Dash to reward owners of these servers for maintaining a service for the blockchain.

title: Max Supply
excerpt: The best approximation of the maximum amount of coins that will ever exist in the lifetime of the cryptocurrency. *see Circulating Supply and Total Supply.

title: Maximal Extractable Value (MEV)
excerpt: Maximal extractable value (MEV) is a measure of the profit a miner can make through their ability to arbitrarily include, exclude, or re-order transactions within the blocks they produce.

title: Medium of Exchange
excerpt: A medium of exchange can be seen as an intermediary instrument or system that is used to facilitate a sale, purchase or even trade of goods between parties.

title: Megahashes Per Second
excerpt: Megahashes per second (MH/s) is a unit of measurement that equals one million hashes per second.

title: Meme Economy
excerpt: Meme Economy is a satirical online subculture in which memes are addressed in financial language as if they were commodities or capital assets with varying prices.

title: Memecoin
excerpt: Memecoins are the crypto tokens created as a joke or meme and claim to offer huge gains to holders.

title: Memorandum of Understanding (MoU)
excerpt: A Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) is defined as a written agreement between two or more parties, which is legally-non binding.

title: Mempool
excerpt: A mempool is the node's collection of all of the unconfirmed transactions that it has seen.

title: Mercenary Capital
excerpt: Mercenary capital refers to the opportunistic capital provided by investors seeking to take advantage of the short-term incentive programs conducted by a platform for individual gain.

title: Merkle Tree
excerpt: A tree structure in cryptography, in which every leaf node is labelled with the hash of a data block and every non-leaf node is labelled with the cryptographic hash of the labels of its child nodes. Hash trees allow efficient and secure verification of the contents of blockchains, as each change propagates upwards so verification can be done by simply looking at the top hash.

title: MetaMask
excerpt: An online digital wallet that allows users to manage, transfer and receive Ethereum, operating as an extension to a regular browser.

title: Metatransaction
excerpt: A metatransaction executes a transaction signed by another party on behalf of the original signer, ferrying this original transaction onto the public blockchain rather than the person having to do it themselves

title: Metaverse
excerpt: A metaverse is a digital universe that contains all the aspects of the real world, such as real-time interactions and economies. It offers a unique experience to end-users.

title: Metaverse-as-a-Service (MaaS)
excerpt: Metaverse-as-a-Service (MaaS) is a novel technological solution that anyone can use to build their own metaverses. The service provider offers the necessary infrastructure to enable both developers and non-technical users to create immersive virtual experiences alongside additional features like in-game tokenomics, NFTs, play-to-earn models, and much more.

title: Metcalfe’s Law
excerpt: Metcalfe’s Law is a way of describing the use of networks and highlights that the more users a network has, the more valuable it is to each individual user.

title: MEV Protection
excerpt: MEV protection refers to strategies and tools designed to shield crypto transactions from Maximal Extractable Value (MEV) exploitation.

title: Micro Cap
excerpt: A digital asset with a very small market capitalization is referred to as a micro-cap stock/asset/cryptocurrency in the financial sector.

title: MicroBitcoin (uBTC)
excerpt: One millionth of a bitcoin or 0.000001 of a bitcoin. Often confused as a fork of Bitcoin.

title: Microchain
excerpt: Microchains are lightweight chains that operate in parallel within a common set of validators. In a microchain-based network, the role of proposing blocks is separate from the role of validating them.

title: Micropayment
excerpt: A micropayment is essentially a small transaction that is carried out online and can be as small as a fraction of a cent.

title: Microtransaction
excerpt: A business model where very small payments can be made in exchange for common digital goods and services, such as pages of an ebook or items in a game.

title: Mid Cap
excerpt: Mid cap is a measure derived from a mathematical computation that determines the market value of a cryptocurrency with a market capitalization ranging from $1 billion to $10 billion.

title: MilliBitcoin
excerpt: MilliBitcoin, also known as mBTC, is a sub-unit of Bitcoin which is equivalent to the one-thousandth of a BTC, the most famous and valuable cryptocurrency in the world.

title: Mimetic Theory
excerpt: Mimetic theory explains human behavior and culture, and when understood contextually in economics, it deals with how things become desirable to individuals.

title: Mineable
excerpt: Some cryptocurrencies have a system through which miners can be rewarded with newly-created cryptocurrencies for creating blocks through contributing their hash power. Cryptocurrencies with this ability to generate new cryptocurrencies through the process of confirmation is said to be mineable. * Not Mineable Some cryptocurrencies are generated only through other mechanisms, such as annual inflation through staking. These cryptocurrencies are said to be not mineable.

title: Minecraft
excerpt: Minecraft is a video game where players can essentially create and break apart different kinds of blocks in a three-dimensional world.

title: Miners
excerpt: Contributors to a blockchain taking part in the process of mining. They can be professional miners or organizations with large-scale operations, or hobbyists who set up mining rigs at home or in the office.

title: Minimum Collateralization Ratio (MCR)
excerpt: Minimum Collateralization Ratio (MCR) is the least amount of collateral that needs to be pledged against a given loan.

title: Minimum Viable Product (MVP)
excerpt: A minimum viable product (MVP) is a product that has enough features to attract early-adopter customers and validate a product idea.

title: Mining
excerpt: Cryptocurrency mining is a process where blocks are added to a blockchain, verifying transactions. It is also the process through which new Bitcoin and *some* altcoins are created.

title: Mining Algorithm
excerpt: An integral component of blockchain, a mining algorithm is the set of rules or instructions that a computer follows to generate a valid block.

title: Mining as a Service (MaaS)
excerpt: Cloud mining or mining-as-a-service allows users to rent the mining capacity of hardware in companies.

title: Mining Contract
excerpt: Another term for cloud mining, where users can rent or invest in mining capacity online.

title: Mining Difficulty
excerpt: The mining difficulty of a cryptocurrency is how difficult it is to find the right hash for the next block.

title: Mining Farm
excerpt: A mining farm is when a group of miners mine together for a variety of advantageous reasons, like energy use.

title: Mining Pool
excerpt: An arrangement where a number of miners pool their resources to increase their chances of finding the next block.

title: Mining Reward
excerpt: The income that miners receive after finding and validating a block.

title: Mining Rewards
excerpt: Mining rewards are the rewards that crypto miners receive for mining a new block on the blockchain.

title: Mining Rig
excerpt: Equipment that is used for mining cryptocurrencies.

title: Minnow
excerpt: An alternative term to Fish, describing someone with a small crypto investment.

title: Minting
excerpt: Minting is the process of generating new coins using the proof-of-stake mechanism and adding them to the circulation to be traded.

title: Mnemonic Phrase
excerpt: A list of words used in sequence to access or restore your crypto assets.

title: Mnemonics
excerpt: Mnemonics are memory aids with a system such as letters or associations that help in recall. *see Mnemonic Phrase.

title: Mobile Wallet
excerpt: A mobile wallet is a crypto wallet installed on a mobile device.

title: Modular Proof Verification
excerpt: Modular proof verification is a process where confirming the validity and correctness of a ZK Proof, one of the most expensive parts of running ZK rollups and ZK apps, is offloaded to a dedicated service.

title: Moloch DAO
excerpt: Moloch DAO can refer to the DAO framework, a description of a DAO that uses the framework, or the name of the Ethereum grant-giving DAO that originally created the framework.

title: Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS)
excerpt: The Monetary Authority of Singapore is the central bank managing money supply and regulating interest rates, inflation rate, and value for Singaporean currency.

title: Monetary Policy
excerpt: Monetary policy can be defined as a set of rules that a country’s central bank makes to control the supply of money.

title: Money
excerpt: Money is a widely used medium of exchange. It facilitates trade and gives people a way to store wealth.

title: Money Flow Index (MFI)
excerpt: The Money Flow Index (MFI) is a technical indicator that measures the buying or selling pressure of an asset through price and volume.

title: Money Laundering
excerpt: Money laundering is a technique used for illegal businesses to hide their money from the authorities.

title: Money Market
excerpt: A financial market where short-term lending or borrowing takes place.

title: Money Transfer License
excerpt: Money transmitter businesses need to acquire MTL or Money Transmitter License to be able to operate legally.

title: Money Transmitter
excerpt: A business entity that provides money transfer services or payment instruments.

title: Monopoly
excerpt: ‍Monopoly is a type of market structure in which there is only one seller of a particular good or service.

title: Moon
excerpt: A situation where there is a continuous upward movement in the price of a cryptocurrency. Often used in communities to question when a cryptocurrency will experience such a phenomenon, saying “When moon?” It is usually combined with “When Lambo?”

title: Moore's Law
excerpt: Moore's Law states that computers' speed and capability will increase every year, even as cost goes down.

title: Motoko Programming Language (DFINITY)
excerpt: The programming language for developing projects to run on the Internet Computer blockchain.

title: Move (Programming Language)
excerpt: Move is a programming language initially used to build the Diem blockchain. It was created by the Diem Association, which was a tech consortium backed by Meta.

title: Move-to-Earn
excerpt: One of the newest blockchain-powered innovations, move-to-earn is a concept that encourages users to stay physically active by incentivizing them with crypto-based rewards.

title: Moving Average (MA)
excerpt: Moving Average (MA) is a technical indicator that reacts to the trends of the financial markets and is used by market experts to predict the direction of an asset's trend.

title: Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD)
excerpt: A technical analysis method.

title: Mt. Gox
excerpt: A crypto exchange for buying and selling Bitcoin that closed in 2014 after a major hack.

title: Multi-Chain
excerpt: Multi-chain is a foundational term that promotes blockchain interoperability and decentralization.

title: Multi-Coin Wallet
excerpt: A multi-coin wallet, also known as a multi-chain wallet, allows users to keep more than one crypto asset belonging to multiple blockchain networks.

title: Multi-level Marketing
excerpt: Multi-level marketing is a business model that involves selling products on behalf of a company by unsalaried and hierarchical sales teams – earning commissions based on their sales and recruiting efforts.

title: Multi-Party Computation
excerpt: Multi-party computation (MPC) is a cryptographic mechanism that distributes computation operations across several parties where no singular entity can see the other parties’ data.

title: Multi-Party Computation as-a-Service
excerpt: Multi-party computation (MPC) as-a-service is a business model where consumers (enterprises and individuals) can rent MPC nodes from an MPC service provider instead of outright buying them or building them from scratch.

title: Multi-Signature (Multi-Sig)
excerpt: An added layer of security by requiring more than one key to authorize a transaction.

title: Multiple Data Availability (MultiDA)
excerpt: This blockchain architecture uses more than one data availability (DA) service to ensure data redundancy.

title: Multisignature
excerpt: Multisignature crypto wallets require more than a single signature to sign a transaction.

title: Mutual Credit Line
excerpt: Mutual credit is a multilateral exchange network in which money created endogenously within the network serves as a medium of exchange.

title: My Story (VeChain)
excerpt: A digital assurance system built on a blockchain by DNV and VeChain.

title: Name Wrapper
excerpt: Name Wrapper is a smart contract that allows all registered ENS names to be converted into NFTs, expanding their customizability.

title: Negative Volume Index (NVI)
excerpt: The negative volume index (NVI) is a powerful technical indicator that shows how price is being influenced by low-volume timeframes.

title: Network
excerpt: A network refers to all nodes in the operation of a blockchain at any given moment in time.

title: Network Latency
excerpt: Network latency refers to the amount of time it takes for a computer on one network to communicate with a computer on another network.

title: Network-Enhanced Virtual Machine (NEVM)
excerpt: A Network-Enhanced Virtual Machine (NEVM) combines the best attributes of the Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH) networks, enabling smart contracts to leverage unprecedented levels of interoperability and scalability to support broad mass adoption.

title: Newb
excerpt: A newb is someone that is new to a certain industry.

title: NFT Royalties
excerpt: NFT royalties are a way for creators to earn a percentage of the sale value each time their NFT is sold on the secondary market.

title: NGMI
excerpt:
NGMI stands for "Not Gonna Make It," and is a slang that's become increasingly popular in the cryptocurrency community. 

At its core, NGMI is a dismissive and pessimistic expression used to convey doubt, skepticism, or a lack of confidence in something – whether it's a particular investment, a crypto project, or even someone's trading strategy.

title: Nick Szabo
excerpt: Nick Szabo is the inventor of Bit Gold and the use of smart contracts.

title: Nifty Gateway
excerpt: Nifty Gateway is an NFT platform owned by the Winklevoss twins.

title: No-Coiner
excerpt: A no-coiner is someone who has no cryptocurrency in his or her investment portfolio and firmly believes that cryptocurrency in general will fail.

title: Node
excerpt: The most basic unit of blockchain infrastructure that stores data.

title: Node.js
excerpt: Node.js is a cross-platform JavaScript runtime environment that can be used for both servers and desktop apps.

title: Nominators
excerpt: Nominators are one of two main actors who are involved in a blockchain network that uses the nominated proof-of-stake (NPoS) consensus algorithm.

title: Non-Custodial
excerpt: Usually referring to the storage of keys, in relation to wallets or exchanges, a non-custodial setup is one in which private keys are held by the user directly.

title: Non-fungible Assets
excerpt: The term "non-fungible assets" describes non-fungibility within a collection of similar assets being issued by a single party.

title: Non-Fungible Token (NFT)
excerpt: Non-fungible tokens (NFTs) are cryptocurrencies that do not possess the property of fungibility.

title: Nonce
excerpt: When a transaction is hashed by a miner, an arbitrary number meant to be used only once is generated, called a nonce.

title: Nonce Error
excerpt: A nonce error occurs when a nonce - a number used only once - is misused or mishandled in a system.

title: Notarization on Blockchain
excerpt: Notarization on blockchain simply uses the intrinsic nature and advantages of blockchain technology to let anyone create a timestamped artifact. The authorship and identity of this artifact can easily and securely be identified at any point.

title: Odysee
excerpt: Odysee is a server-less video hosting and distribution platform built on top of the LBRY protocol.

title: Off-Chain
excerpt: A transaction that is processed outside the blockchain network with an increased speed and reduced cost.

title: Off-Chain Governance
excerpt: Off-chain governance is a type of blockchain governance in which decisions are made informally, away from the primary code base of the blockchain.

title: Off-Chain Transaction
excerpt: An off-chain transaction is defined as a second-layer protocol where the transactions occur on a network and move value outside of the blockchain.

title: Off-Ledger Currency
excerpt: A currency that is created (minted) outside of the specified blockchain ledger but is accepted or used.

title: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC)
excerpt: The Office of the Comptroller (OCC) is a U.S. Treasury branch that regulates all national banks, federal savings associations, federal branches and foreign bank agencies

title: Offline Storage
excerpt: The act of storing cryptocurrencies in devices or systems not connected to the internet.

title: Offshore Account
excerpt: An offshore account is defined as a foreign bank account with assets and investments set outside of your origin country or country of residence.

title: OHM Fork
excerpt: OlympusDAO or Ohm Forks represent the upgrades to OlympusDAO’s codebase that has given birth to a variety of forked products.

title: Omnichain
excerpt: Omnichain refers to a blockchain infrastructure that leverages chain abstraction to facilitate seamless interaction, data transfer, and transaction settlement across multiple blockchains.

title: On-Balance Volume (OBV)
excerpt: On-balance volume (OBV) is a technical trading indicator that forecasts an asset's price movements based on the volume flow.

title: On-Chain
excerpt: Transactions that are recorded on the blockchain itself and shared with all of the participants are done on-chain.

title: On-Chain Governance
excerpt: On-chain governance is a decentralized framework used for organizing and integrating updates/improvements to the blockchain networks.

title: On-Ledger Currency
excerpt: A currency that is both minted on the blockchain ledger and also used on the blockchain ledger, such as Bitcoin.

title: Onchain Fiat
excerpt: Onchain fiat is a first-of-its-kind, fully authorized and regulated subset of stablecoins that allows for seamless transitions between traditional bank accounts and web3.

title: One Cancels the Other Order (OCO)
excerpt: A situation where two orders for cryptocurrency are placed simultaneously, with a rule in place to enforce that if one is accepted, the other is cancelled.

title: Online Storage
excerpt: The act of storing cryptocurrencies in devices or systems connected to the internet.

title: Ontorand Consensus Engine (Ontology)
excerpt: Ontorand Consensus Engine is the VBFT consensus mechanism on the Ontology blockchain.

title: Open Finance (OpenFi)
excerpt: OpenFi, short for "Open Finance," is a financial framework that integrates traditional finance (TradFi) with decentralized finance (DeFi).

title: Open Interest
excerpt: Open interest refers to the total number of outstanding derivative contracts, specifically futures and options, that are held by market participants at the end of each trading session.

title: Open Source
excerpt: Open source is a philosophy, with participants believing in the free and open sharing of information in pursuit of the greater common good.

title: Open/Close
excerpt: The price at which a cryptocurrency opens at a time period or the programming principle of software parts being extendable.

title: OpenSea
excerpt: OpenSea is a decentralized P2P platform for NFTs.

title: Opera Mainnet (Fantom)
excerpt: Opera (Fantom) is a permissionless, open-source framework that allows everyone to engage in the network through staking and governance.

title: Operating System (OS)
excerpt: An operating system (OS) is a software and a resource manager that sits between the hardware and the user.

title: Optimistic Oracle
excerpt: An optimistic oracle uses a dispute/arbitration process to ensure data accuracy.  This compares to a price-feed oracle, which relies on nodes to provide consistent price feed data on-chain.

title: Optimistic Rollup
excerpt: An optimistic rollup is a type of layer-2 scaling solution that relies on off-chain computation to record transactions in layer 2 trustlessly.

title: Option
excerpt: A contract giving the buyer the right, but not the obligation, to buy or sell an underlying asset or instrument at a specified strike price.

title: Options Market
excerpt: A public market for options, giving the buyer an option to buy or sell a cryptocurrency at a specific strike price, on or before a specific date.

title: Oracle Extractable Value (OEV)
excerpt: Oracle Extractable Value (OEV) is a type of Maximal Extractable Value (MEV) that oracles can control.

title: Oracle Manipulation
excerpt: Oracle manipulation is when an oracle smart contract is manipulated by hackers.

title: Oracles
excerpt: An agent that finds and verifies information, bridging the real world and the blockchain by providing data to smart contracts for execution of said contracts under specified conditions.

title: Order Book
excerpt: An order book comprises different key information regarding an asset.

title: Orphan
excerpt: A valid block on the blockchain that is not part of the main chain.

title: Orphaned Block
excerpt: An orphaned block is a block where the parent block does not exist or is unknown.

title: Ouroboros Praos
excerpt: Ouroboros Praos is a proof-of-stake consensus mechanism developed by IOHK and is an updated version of Ouroboros Classic.

title: Over-Collateralization
excerpt: Over-collateralization (OC) is the provision of collateral that is worth more than enough to cover potential losses in cases of default.

title: Over-the-Counter (OTC)
excerpt: Over-the-counter is defined as a transaction made outside of an exchange, often peer-to-peer through private trades.

title: Over-the-Counter (OTC) Trading
excerpt: Over-the-counter refers to the process of how securities are traded through a broker-dealer network as opposed to a centralized exchange.

title: Overbought
excerpt: When a cryptocurrency has been purchased by more and more investors over time, with its price increasing for an extended period of time.

title: Oversold
excerpt: When a cryptocurrency has been sold by more and more investors over time, with its price decreasing for an extended period of time.

title: P2P Bridge
excerpt: A P2P bridge is a feature on decentralized exchanges (DEX) that enables two users to swap the same cryptocurrency across two blockchain protocols without involving a third party.

title: P2P DEX
excerpt: P2P DEX, or peer-to-peer decentralized exchange, is a blockchain-based application that supports P2P trading.

title: P2P Trading
excerpt: Peer-to-peer (P2P) trading involves decentralized transactions where two users swap cryptocurrencies directly with each other. Both buyers and sellers interact without the involvement of a third party.

title: Pair
excerpt: Trade between one cryptocurrency and another, for example, the trading pair BTC/ETH.

title: Paper Trading
excerpt: Paper trading or simulated trading is the practice of using a virtual transactional environment to simulate trading without the use of real capital.

title: Paper Wallet
excerpt: A physical document containing your private key or seed phrase.

title: Parachain
excerpt: Parachains are application-specific data structures that run in parallel to each other within Polkadot.

title: Participation Node
excerpt: Participation nodes are present in the Algorand platform to help in conducting the Pure Proof of Stake (PPoS) consensus process.

title: Passive Income
excerpt: Passive income is money produced from investments that do not require the earner to be actively involved.

title: Password Manager
excerpt: A password manager is a tool or software that stores all sorts of passwords needed for online applications and services.

title: Paul Le Roux
excerpt: Paul Le Roux is a criminal kingpin that many believe could be the founder of Bitcoin, Satoshi Nakamoto.

title: Payee
excerpt: A payee is a party within an exchange of goods or even services that can receive payment.

title: Peer-to-Peer (P2P)
excerpt: The decentralized interactions between parties in a distributed network, partitioning tasks or workloads between peers.

title: Peer-to-Peer (P2P) Lending
excerpt: Crypto P2P lending refers to a practice of lending assets without the involvement of a middleman. Such loans rely on collateral material originally owned by borrowers.

title: Peg
excerpt: A “peg” is a specified price for the rate of exchange between two assets.

title: Pegged Currency
excerpt: A stablecoin is a currency whose value is pegged to a real-world asset, such as a fiat currency.

title: Permissioned Ledger
excerpt: A ledger designed with restrictions, such that only people or organizations requiring access have permission to access it.

title: Permissionless
excerpt: Often used to describe blockchains, a system is said to be permissionless when there is no entity that can regulate who can use it and how it can be used.

title: Permissionless Market Creation
excerpt: Permissionless market creation refers to a system in which anyone can set up a financial market that facilitates the exchange of funds or assets between two or more parties without needing approval.

title: Perpetual Contracts
excerpt: A perpetual contract is a derivative similar to a futures contract but without an expiry date.

title: Perpetual Futures
