excerpt: Perpetual futures are like futures (derivative contracts or agreements to buy or sell a commodity at a specified price) but without an expiration date. They allow traders to leverage or hedge indefinitely, popular applications of this include Ethereum or Bitcoin.

title: Phishing
excerpt: When a scammer pretends to be a trusted institution or person to trick people into revealing sensitive information such as Social Security numbers, passwords, banking details, etc., often through a malware link disguised as legitimate.

title: Phone Phishing
excerpt: Phone phishing, also known as a telephone scam or vishing (voice phishing), refers to the practice of using fraudulent and malicious phone calls to extort money or sensitive information from victims.

title: Physical Bitcoins
excerpt: A physical Bitcoin is a physical token that usually has an intricate design, as well as a public key and private key.

title: Platform
excerpt: On CoinMarketCap, platform refers to the parent blockchain of tokens. It may also refer to a cryptocurrency exchange on which you may trade cryptocurrencies.

title: Play-to-Earn (Play2Earn)
excerpt: The play-to-earn business model supports the notion of an open economy and gives financial rewards to players who bring value to its metaverse.

title: Play2Earn (Play-to-Earn)
excerpt: The play-to-earn business model supports the notion of an open economy and gives financial rewards to players who bring value to its metaverse.

title: Player Payout
excerpt: Player payouts is a new way of automatically paying online gaming participants immediately after the tournament ends.

title: Plutus (Cardano)
excerpt: The scripting language used on Cardano blockchain for smart contract development.

title: Politeia (Decred)
excerpt: A decentralized governance platform that allows Decred stakeholders to submit, track and discuss proposals for suggestion and implementation of new ideas.

title: Ponzi Scheme
excerpt: A fraudulent investment involving the payment of purported returns to existing investors from funds contributed by new investors.

title: Portfolio
excerpt: A collection of cryptocurrencies or crypto assets held by an investment company, hedge fund, financial institution or individual.

title: Portfolio Tracking
excerpt: The act of tracking the movement and performance of assets of your financial holdings is termed portfolio tracking. This monitoring activity can cover stocks, commodities, mutual funds, ETFs, cryptocurrencies and NFTs

title: Position Size
excerpt: Position size is a vital part of a trading strategy, since it is directly related to one’s potential profit or loss. Sizing a position also plays an important role in risk management as knowing when to execute larger or smaller trades, and when to increase or reduce the size of a position impacts profitability.

title: Post-Mine
excerpt: The retroactive creation of new coins following a cryptocurrency’s launch, before public mining is possible.

title: Pre-IDO
excerpt: Pre-IDO refers to token offerings before the actual initial DEX offering (IDO) takes place.

title: Pre-Mine
excerpt: When some or all of a coin’s initial supply is generated during or before the public launch.

title: Pre-Sale
excerpt: The sale of a cryptocurrency, ahead of it going public, to specific investors.

title: Prediction Market
excerpt: Prediction Markets are exchange-traded markets where the future outcomes of events are traded. It indicates the confidence of the crowd in a specific future event.

title: Price Impact
excerpt: The difference between market price and estimated price due to trade size.

title: Private Blockchain
excerpt: A private blockchain is a type of blockchain in which only a single organization has authority over the network.

title: Private Key/Secret Key
excerpt: A piece of code generated in asymmetric-key encryption process, paired with a public key, to be used in decrypting information hashed with the public key.

title: Procedural Programming
excerpt: Procedural programming refers to a series of instructions that inform a computer what it should do step-by-step to achieve the task.

title: Profit and Loss (P&L) Statement
excerpt: A profit and loss (P&L) statement is a financial document that gives that sum up the earnings, costs, and expenditures incurred during a specified period.

title: Programmability
excerpt: Programmability implies that something is capable of following instructions.

title: Programmable Privacy
excerpt: Programmable privacy redefines data protection in decentralized applications (dApps), offering a flexible concept that empowers users and developers to personalize privacy settings.

title: Proof Market
excerpt: A proof market is a decentralized marketplace where users can buy and sell cryptographic proofs to verify ownership, the validity of a particular transaction and the authenticity of a piece of information or computation results.

title: Proof of Attendance Protocol
excerpt: Proof of Attendance Protocol (POAP) refers to a business offering to use the ERC-721 NFT protocol on Ethereum to ascribe individuals a unique, non-fungible blockchain-based identifier that only the person can access

title: Proof of Reserves (PoR)
excerpt: Proof of Reserves (PoR) is a method of using cryptographic verification to demonstrate possession of digital assets.

title: Proof of Stake Authority (PoSA)
excerpt: Proof of Stake Authority is an innovative consensus algorithm that represents a hybrid of Proof-of-Stake and Proof-of-Authority.

title: Proof-of-Authority (PoA)
excerpt: A blockchain consensus mechanism that delivers comparatively fast transactions using identity as a stake.

title: Proof-of-Burn
excerpt: Proof-of-burn is an attempt at creating a system that can prevent fraudulent transactions on a blockchain and can also improve the overall efficiency and functioning of the blockchain.

title: Proof-of-Burn (PoB)
excerpt: A blockchain consensus mechanism aiming to bootstrap one blockchain to another with increased energy efficiency, by verifying that a cost was incurred in “burning” a coin by sending it to an unspendable address.

title: Proof-of-Developer (PoD)
excerpt: Any verification that provides evidence of a real, living software developer who created a cryptocurrency, in order to prevent an anonymous developer from making away with any raised funds without delivering a working model.

title: Proof-of-Donation
excerpt: Proof-of-donation refers to the integration of charitable donations into the functionality of a blockchain.

title: Proof-of-History (PoH)
excerpt: Proof of History (PoH) nodes have internal clocks that validate events and time. The incoming events are hashed using a verifiable delay function, also known as VDF, that increases the speed and scalability of a blockchain.

title: Proof-of-Immutability (PoIM)
excerpt: Proof-of-Immutability (PoIM) is a novel blockchain architecture designed to protect data privacy in a distributed network. PoIM enables the blockchain to persist data in a decentralized and provably immutable manner, without having to distribute the data among nodes.

title: Proof-of-Replication
excerpt: Proof-of-replication (PoRep) is the way that a storage miner proves to the network that they are storing an entirely unique copy of a piece of data.

title: Proof-of-Spacetime
excerpt: In simplest terms, PoSt means that someone can now guarantee that they are spending a certain amount of space for storage.

title: Proof-of-Stake (PoS)
excerpt: A blockchain consensus mechanism in addition to Proof-of-Work that maintains the integrity of blockchain.

title: Proof-of-Time (PoT)
excerpt: Proof-of-Time (PoT) is a decentralized, scalable, verifiably secure, and environmentally-friendly consensus algorithm that checks event data by choosing validators based on their ranking scores and a fixed stake.

title: Proof-of-Validation
excerpt: Proof-of-validation (PoV) is a unique proof-of-stake (PoS) consensus mechanism that works to achieve consensus through staked validator nodes.

title: Proof-of-Work (PoW)
excerpt: A blockchain consensus mechanism involving solving of computationally intensive puzzles to validate transactions and create new blocks. *see Proof-of-Stake (PoS).

title: Protocol
excerpt: The set of rules that define interactions on a network, usually involving consensus, transaction validation, and network participation on a blockchain.

title: Protocol Layer
excerpt: A protocol layer of the blockchain is defined as the rules and processes that govern how the network will operate. This is where we can find the different algorithms that determine how consensus is achieved and who gets to create new blocks.

title: Pseudonymous
excerpt: Writing under a false name, such as “Satoshi Nakamoto.”

title: Public Address
excerpt: A public address is the cryptographic hash of a public key, allowing the user to use it as an address to request for payment.

title: Public Blockchain
excerpt: A blockchain that can be accessed by anyone.

title: Public Key
excerpt: A public key refers to a series of alphanumeric characters used to encrypt plain text messages into ciphertext.

title: Public Sale
excerpt: A public sale is the final stage of an ICO in which a company offers its token to the public at a significant discount before the token is listed on cryptocurrency exchanges.

title: Public-Key Cryptography
excerpt: Public-key cryptography is a collection of algorithms-based cryptographic procedures that are used to jumble secret data and make it look randomized.

title: Public-Key Infrastructure
excerpt: A public key infrastructure (PKI) is a collection of roles, rules, hardware, software, and processes for creating, managing, distributing, using, storing, and revoking digital certificates, as well as managing public-key encryption.

title: Pump and Dump (P&D) Scheme
excerpt: A form of fraud involving the artificial inflation of the price of a cryptocurrency with false and misleading positive statements.

title: Pure Proof of Stake (PPoS)
excerpt: Pure Proof of Stake (PPoS) is Algorand’s consensus mechanism that allows random selection of validators based on the consistency of their stakes.

title: Put Option
excerpt: A put option contract offers an owner the opportunity, but not the compulsion, to buy an underlying security at a certain price within a given time frame.

title: Pyramid Scheme
excerpt: A pyramid scheme is a scam with a hierarchical top-down structure.

title: QR Code
excerpt: A machine-readable label that shows information encoded into a graphical black-and-white pattern.

title: Quant Zone (FTX Exchange)
excerpt: A tool to create and share trading strategies on the FTX exchange.

title: Quantum Bit (Qubit)
excerpt: A unit of measurement for the number of bits in quantum information, and is also called a "qubit."

title: Quantum Computing
excerpt: A computer that harnesses phenomena from quantum mechanics in order to perform much more efficient computations than older, classical computer technologies are capable of.

title: Quasar Smart Contract (OMG Foundation)
excerpt: A smart contract by OMG Network to solve layer-2 blockchain problems.

title: Quorum (Governance
excerpt: A quorum is the minimum number of members of an assembly or group that must be present at any of its meetings to make the proceedings of that meeting valid.

title: Radio Frequency Identification (RFID)
excerpt: Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) is a type of technology that uses radio waves to passively identify a tagged item or individual.

title: Rage-quit
excerpt: Rage-quit is the process where a member of a DAO exits part or all of their stake, leaves with a proportional share of the assets in the DAO’s treasury, and quits their participation.

title: Raiden Network
excerpt: An off-chain scaling solution aiming to enable near-instant, low-fee and scalable payments on the Ethereum blockchain, similar to Bitcoin's proposed Lightning Network.

title: Rank
excerpt: The relative position of a cryptocurrency by market capitalization.

title: Ransomware
excerpt: Ransomware is a type of malware used by hackers to steal or encrypt their victims’ files to extort them for a ransom in exchange for file decryption or restoration.

title: Real World Assets (RWAs)
excerpt: Real World Assets are off-chain assets, which are tokenized and brought on-chain for use in DeFi. Tokenization involves converting an asset's value into a digital token for representation and transactions on the blockchain.

title: Rebalancing
excerpt: Rebalancing is the process of realigning the weightage of a portfolio of assets,  that involves buying or selling assets periodically to maintain a targeted level of asset allocation and risk.

title: Rebase
excerpt: A token designed so that the circulating supply adjusts automatically according to price fluctuations.

title: Recovery Seed
excerpt: A recovery seed is a cryptographically derived security code composed of a list of random words, typically ranging between 12 and 14.

title: Recursion
excerpt: Recursion refers to when a function calls on itself directly or indirectly in a circular loop(s).

title: Redundancy
excerpt: The term refers to something that is in excess of that required for normal operation.

title: Regenerative Economy
excerpt: A regenerative economy is a circular economic system that benefits the environment and society as a whole.

title: Regenerative Finance (ReFi)
excerpt: Regenerative Finance can be defined as a system that regenerates its resource capacity over time.

title: Regens
excerpt: Regen, like degen, is a term used to describe a crypto user who jumps into ReFi communities or invests in tokens that use blockchain technology to advance positive environmental impact.

title: Regional/Local/Community Currencies
excerpt: A local currency is one that is spent in a certain geographical location. A regional currency refers to a local currency utilized in a larger area, and a community currency is often used within a specific community as a means of exchange.

title: Regulated
excerpt: Regulation is when something is controlled by a specific set of rules.

title: Regulatory Compliance
excerpt: Regulatory compliance is a set of mandates that every company or industry is required to follow to track accountability at work.

title: Rehypothecation
excerpt: Rehypothecation is the practice where banks, and even the brokers themselves, use assets that have been posted as collateral by their clients for their own purposes.

title: Rehypothecation
excerpt: Rehypothecation is a process where brokers use the assets collateralized by clients for their own benefit.

title: REKT
excerpt: A shorthand slang for “wrecked,” describing a bad loss in a trade.

title: Relative Strength Index (RSI)
excerpt: A form of technical analysis that serves as a momentum oscillator, measuring the speed and change of price movements.

title: Relay Chain
excerpt: The Relay Chain is the central chain that is used by the Polkadot network.

title: Relay Nodes
excerpt: Relay nodes help block-producing nodes communicate by guaranteeing that the authenticity of the core nodes and the blockchain is preserved, even if one or more relays are hacked.

title: Renewable Energy
excerpt: Renewable energy is derived from solar, wind, and other ‘indefinite’ resources or operations that are renewed on a regular basis.

title: Repair Miners
excerpt: Repair miners are a proposed type of mining node within the Filecoin network.

title: Replay Attack
excerpt: Replay attacks are network security attacks where the comms between a sender and receiver is intercepted.

title: Replicated Ledger
excerpt: A copy of a distributed ledger in a network that is distributed to all participants in a cryptocurrency network.

title: Replicated Security (RS)
excerpt: Replicated Security (RS) is a new technology that lets a Cosmos blockchain share its economic security with another using the Inter-Blockchain Communication protocol (IBC).

title: Resistance (Line/Level)
excerpt: The highest price level of an asset during a specific period.

title: Restaking
excerpt: Restaking is a mechanism that allows validators on proof-of-stake (PoS) blockchain networks to redeploy their staked cryptocurrency across other PoS-based chains.

title: Retargeting
excerpt: A retargeting algorithm, also referred to as a difficulty adjustment algorithm, is used on proof-of-work blockchains, such as Bitcoin.

title: Revenue Participation Tokens
excerpt: Revenue participation tokens are a two token system that uses one participation token and one payout token.

title: Reverse ICO
excerpt: In a reverse ICO, an already-established company raises funds by selling tokens to shift its structure to decentralization.

title: Reverse Indicator
excerpt: A person whom you may use as an indicator of how not to place buy or sell orders because they are always wrong at predicting price movements of cryptocurrencies.

title: Ring CT (Confidential Transactions)
excerpt: RingCT is how transaction amounts are hidden in Monero.

title: Ring Miners
excerpt: Ring miners are network participants in the Loopring protocol who manage order rings and ensure trades are completed for all parties involved.

title: Ring Signature
excerpt: A cryptographic digital signature that obfuscates the identities of two parties within a transaction.

title: Roadmap
excerpt: A roadmap is a high-level visual summary that helps map out the vision as well as the direction of a specific product.

title: Roger Ver
excerpt: Roger Ver, also known as Bitcoin Jesus, has been a long-term proponent of Bitcoin and Bitcoin Cash.

title: ROI
excerpt: Short for “Return on Investment,” the ratio between the net profit and cost of investing.

title: Rollups-as-a-Service (RaaS)
excerpt: Rollups-as-a-Service (RaaS) allows builders to build and launch their own rollups quickly. RaaS providers deliver end-to-end solutions like customizations of the underlying tech stack, no-code management, and one-click custom integrations with core infrastructure.

title: Roth IRA
excerpt: Roth IRAs are generally the best investment option when you think your taxes will be higher in retirement than they are now.

title: Rough Consensus
excerpt: Rough consensus, in its bare essence, refers to a method of decision-making that doesn't necessarily require unanimity.

title: Ruby (Programming Language)
excerpt: Ruby is a high-level programming language designed with a focus on simplicity and code readability.

title: Rug Pull
excerpt: A rug pull is a type of scam where developers abandon a project and take their investors' money.

title: Rust
excerpt: Rust is a multi-paradigm programming language, similar to C++.

title: Ryuk Ransomware
excerpt: Ryuk ransomware is a ransomware attack first discovered in August 2018.

title: S&P 500 (Standard and Poor's 500)
excerpt: The Standard and Poor's 500, also known as S&P 500, is a stock market index that represents a list of 500 public companies located in the U.S and their performance in the market.

title: Satoshi (SATS)
excerpt: The smallest unit of bitcoin with a value of 0.00000001 BTC.

title: Satoshi Nakamoto
excerpt: The individual or group of individuals that created Bitcoin.

title: Scaling Problem
excerpt: The scaling problem is the limitations of a blockchain's transaction throughout and ability to have fast and low cast transactions.

title: Scaling Solution
excerpt: A scaling solution is a method of enable a system to expand.

title: Scam
excerpt: A scheme that is designed to dupe people out of cash or crypto.

title: Scamcoin
excerpt: Coins that are created as "get rich quick schemes" by their developers are referred to as a scamcoin.

title: Scammer
excerpt: A scammer is someone that participates in a fraudulent scheme.

title: Scholarship/Scholar
excerpt: A scholarship is a popular practice in the Axie Infinity universe where managers lend their free Axies to new players (scholars) and earn passively from the battle rewards.

title: Script
excerpt: A computer script is a list of commands that are executed by a certain program or scripting language.

title: Scripting Programming Language
excerpt: A scripting language is a programming language that does not require the compilation step. It uses a high-level construct to carry out one command at a time.

title: Scrypt
excerpt: An alternative proof-of-work (PoW) algorithm to SHA-256, used in Bitcoin mining. Scrypt mining relies more heavily on memory than on pure CPU power, aiming to reduce the advantage that ASICs have and hence increasing network participation and energy efficiency.

title: Second-Layer Solutions
excerpt: A set of solutions built on top of a public blockchain to extend its scalability and efficiency, especially for micro-transactions or actions. Examples include Plasma, TrueBit, Lightning Network and more.

title: Secondary Market
excerpt: A secondary market is a place where investors or traders can buy and sell different kinds of assets or securities that they own, with others.

title: Secure Asset Fund for Users (SAFU)
excerpt: SAFU is the acronym for the "Secure Asset Fund for Users," an insurance fund established by Binance for emergency cases.

title: Secure Element
excerpt: A secure element is a type of hardware chip that runs a specified number of applications.

title: Secure Multi-Party Computation (sMPC)
excerpt: SMPC is a subfield of cryptography that allows parties to compute a function while keeping the inputs private.

title: Secure Proof of Stake (SPoS)
excerpt: Secure Proof of Stake (SPoS) is a consensus mechanism used to secure blockchain networks. It is an evolution of the traditional proof-of-stake (PoS) consensus algorithm

title: Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)
excerpt: An independent agency of the United States federal government, responsible for enforcing federal securities laws, proposing securities rules, and regulating the securities industry, the nation's stock and options exchanges, and other related activities and organizations.

title: Security
excerpt: The term securities refers to a fungible and tradable financial instrument that carries a type of monetary value.

title: Security Token
excerpt: A security token is essentially a digital form of traditional securities.

title: Security Token Offering
excerpt: A security token offering (STO) is a public offering where tokenized digital securities are sold.

title: Seed Funding
excerpt: Seed funding is a type of funding that provides capital to startups in exchange for equity in the company.

title: Seed Phrase
excerpt: A single starting point when deriving keys for a deterministic wallet.

title: Segregated Witness (SegWit)
excerpt: A Bitcoin Improvement Proposal (BIP) that aimed to fix transaction malleability on Bitcoin.

title: Selfish Mining
excerpt: A situation in which a miner mines a new block but does not broadcast this new block to the other miners.

title: Sell Wall
excerpt: A situation where a large limit order has been placed to sell when a cryptocurrency reaches a certain value.

title: Semantic Web
excerpt: The ambition of the Semantic Web is to enable computers to manipulate information on our behalf.

title: Series B Funding
excerpt: A Series B funding round is the second round of funding a business.

title: Settlement

title: White Hat Computer Hacker
excerpt: White hat hackers use their skills to improve security by exposing vulnerabilities before malicious hackers (known as black hat hackers) can detect and exploit them.

title: White Label
excerpt: White Labeling allows a company to customize an existing product framework in order to rebrand and resell this product as their own.

title: White Label Staking
excerpt: In white-label staking, the crypto holders get their validator node explicitly created for them. This is then managed entirely on their behalf by a third-party operator.

title: White Swan Event
excerpt: A white swan is an easily predictable event that is anticipated based on current information and forecasting.

title: Whitelist
excerpt: A list of interested participants in an initial coin offering, who registered their intent to take part or purchase in a sale.

title: Whitepaper
excerpt: A document released by a crypto project that gives investors technical information about its concept, and a roadmap for how it plans to grow and succeed.

title: Winding Down
excerpt: Winding down is the process of unwrapping tokens back to their original form in DeFi.

title: Winding Up
excerpt: In decentralized finance (DeFi), winding up essentially means wrapping crypto tokens through various projects in order to find the best yield.

title: x86 Virtual Machine (Qtum)
excerpt: X86 Virtual Machine enables Qtum developers to write smart contracts in a language of their choice.

title: XBT
excerpt: XBT is the lesser-known ticker symbol for Bitcoin.

title: Yield Curve
excerpt: The yield curve is a line that plots the relationship between yields and maturities of fixed income securities.

title: Yield Farming
excerpt: Yield farming involves earning interest by investing crypto in decentralized finance markets.

title: Yield Sensitivity
excerpt: Yield sensitivity or interest rate sensitivity is a measure of how much a fixed income asset’s price changes due to the fluctuation in interest rates.

title: YTD
excerpt: Stands for Year to Date.

title: Zero Confirmation Transaction
excerpt: Alternative phrasing for an unconfirmed transaction.

title: Zero Confirmation/Unconfirmed Transaction
excerpt: A zero confirmation or unconfirmed transaction is defined as an exchange that has not yet been recorded or verified on the blockchain

title: Zero Knowledge Proof
excerpt: Proving certain information or data is true without revealing it.

title: Zero Knowledge Rollup
excerpt: A zero knowledge rollup is a type of layer 2 scaling solution that relies on zero knowledge cryptography

title: Zero Knowledge Rollups
excerpt: A zero-knowledge rollup is a Layer 2 blockchain solution that performs computations and storage off-chain while funds are held in a smart contract.

title: Zero-Knowledge Proof
excerpt: In cryptography, a zero-knowledge proof enables one party to provide evidence that a transaction or event happened without revealing private details of that transaction or event.

title: Zk-SNARKs
excerpt: A proof that allows one party to prove it owns certain information without revealing it.

title: zkApps
excerpt: zkApps leverage the zero-knowledge proofs (ZKPs), particularly zk-SNARKs and zk-STARKs, to unlock a realm of possibilities that surpass traditional blockchain applications.

title: zkOracle
excerpt: zkOracles utilize Zero Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs) as a trustless medium through which data can pass freely whilst retaining a robust standard of security, privacy and cost-efficiency.

title: zkSharding
excerpt: zkSharding is a re-introduced concept of Ethereum’s sharded execution layer which scales blockchains with parallelized execution within shards secured via zkEVM proofs.

title: zParachain
excerpt: A zParachain is an approach to bridging where a dedicated blockchain in the broader Polkadot ecosystem is used specifically to connect to a network that’s outside the Polkadot ecosystem.
